hub_subtitle: Business English para seguros, reuniões e entrevistas — e o desbloqueio da fala, do primeiro dia ao último

lesson.menu_title: Can You Do This? -- Ability, Permission and Requests
lesson.menu_desc: A aula em que ela descobre que já sabe. Desde a aula 1 o cartão de sobrevivência dela tem "Can you repeat that, please?"; desde a aula 5, "Excuse me, can you help me?" — oito aulas dizendo can sem saber o nome disso. Hoje a caixa abre: uma palavrinha com três empregos (o que eu sei fazer · posso? · você pode?) e uma regra só — depois do can o verbo vem pelado, sem o to. Seis verbos novos (explain, sign, wait, join, print, move), o could como o can de gravata, e o fecho que vale a aula: pedir ajuda sem pedir desculpa antes. -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 9 -- Can You Do This?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Paula Morales | Lesson 9 -- Can You Do This?
lesson.grammar_point: modal can / can't (ability, permission, requests) + could as the polite request form -- bare infinitive after the modal

listenings.text: Hi, Paula. David here. One thing before your call with Ricardo, and it is small, but it changes everything. There is one word in this office that opens every door, and you already use it. You just don't know its name. The word is can. Listen: Can you help me? Can I ask you something? Can you repeat that? Three questions, three different jobs, one word. Here is what it does. First, it says what you are able to do: I can explain the numbers, I can handle the account. Second, it asks for permission: Can I join your call on Friday? Third, and this is the one you need this week: it asks for things. Can you send me the report? Can you print two copies? Now, one warning, and it is the only mistake I want you to avoid today. The verb after can is always naked. Can help. Can send. Never can to help. In your language the verb has a little tail, and the tail wants to come with you. Leave it at home. And one more thing, because I know you are polite: could is just can in a suit. Can you explain that? That is a colleague. Could you explain that? That is a client. Both are correct, and nobody in the history of this company has ever been offended by either one. See you at nine.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. We have the call with the Miami client on Friday, and I want to tell you one thing before it, because I know you are nervous. The client speaks fast. Everybody in Miami speaks fast. It is not you. Here is what I do, and I am the manager: I say, could you repeat that, please? I say it two or three times in every call. Nobody has ever said no. Nobody has ever thought I am bad at my job. Think about it. When a client asks you to repeat something in Portuguese, do you think that person is stupid? No. You repeat it, and the call continues, and nobody remembers it one hour later. It is exactly the same in English. Now the real reason I am calling. I have watched you work for two months. You can handle the account. You can explain the numbers better than I can. You can talk to any broker in this country. There is only one thing you can't do yet, Paula, and it is not English. You can't ask for help without saying sorry first. Stop apologizing. Could you repeat that is a complete sentence. It does not need a sorry in front of it. See you on Friday.

items.situation: Ricardo has the report and you need it before Thursday. He is a colleague you talk to every week -- so keep it simple, and ask.
tips: Can you send me the report?
Ricardo, can you send me the report before Thursday?

items.situation: The Miami client is speaking fast and you caught nothing. This is a client, not a colleague. Ask in the polite way -- and do not say sorry first.
David has a call with Miami on Friday. The account is yours, and you want to be in that room. Ask for permission.
tips: Can I join your call with Miami on Friday?

items.situation: An interviewer asks: "What can you do in your job?" Give two abilities -- full sentences, not one word.
tips: I can handle the account and I can explain the numbers.
I can talk to any broker in the country.

items.situation: Ricardo wants you to sign the document today, but his report is not here yet. Say no -- and say why. This one needs can't.
tips: I can't sign the document today.
I can't sign it without the report. Can you send it first?

items.situation: You need two copies of the report on the table on Thursday morning. Ricardo is doing you a favor -- ask him the polite way.
tips: Could you print two copies for Thursday, please?

bank.label: Asking for things / Pedir as coisas
items: Can you send me the report?
Can you wait a moment, please?

bank.label: Ability and permission / O que eu sei fazer e o que eu posso
items: I can explain the numbers to the client.
I can't sign the document without the report.
